column30:
As a farmer, I want deadline reminders with configurable schedule so notifications match my workflow.



Acceptance Criteria:

- User can configure reminder offsets (for example 14/7/1 days before deadline).

- Reminders respect user timezone settings.

- Disabling reminders immediately stops future reminder triggers.

As a system admin, I want immutable audit export so I can provide evidence during inspections.



Acceptance Criteria:

- Export supports date-range selection and CSV/JSON output.

- Export includes key events: login, profile update, report submit, sync actions.

- Export action itself is audit logged.

As a farmer, I want in-app help ticket entry so I can get support when I am stuck.



Acceptance Criteria:

- User can submit ticket with category, message, and optional screenshot.

- Ticket submission returns confirmation ID to user.

- User can view own ticket history and status.

As a farmer, I want a rule-change feed so I can see what changed in CAP requirements.



Acceptance Criteria:

- Feed lists changes with effective date and summary.

- System tracks read/unread state per user.

- Feed items deep-link to impacted tasks or guidance pages.

As a farmer, I want multilingual UI and content so I can use the app in my preferred language.



Acceptance Criteria:

- User can select language in settings.

- Selected language persists across app restart/login session.

- Release build blocks unresolved translation keys.

As an advisor, I want delegated access to a farmer account so I can help prepare reports.



Acceptance Criteria:

- Farmer can invite and revoke advisor access by email.

- Advisor role supports scoped permissions (read-only, edit).

- All advisor actions are audit logged with actor identity.

As a farmer, I want in-app explanations of penalties and risk level so I can prioritize high-impact tasks.



Acceptance Criteria:

- Each task displays risk level: low, medium, or high.

- Task detail includes plain-language penalty/risk explanation.

- Risk content is configurable from backend content source.

As a farmer, I want a monthly compliance calendar view so I can plan work around deadlines.



Acceptance Criteria:

- Calendar shows deadlines with status-based color coding.

- Tapping a day opens linked compliance tasks.

- Overdue tasks remain visible until completed.

column1: Resolve Sync Conflicts After Offline Mode
column30:
As a farmer, I want conflict resolution after offline sync so I can choose correct values when data differs.



Acceptance Criteria:

- Conflict screen shows local and server values field-by-field.

- User can choose local or server value per conflicting field.

- Final merged result is saved, synced, and audit logged.

As a farmer, I want to duplicate last year’s report so I can submit faster.



Acceptance Criteria:

- Duplicate action creates a new editable draft with copied values.

- Mandatory period/date fields are reset for current cycle.

- Duplicated draft follows normal submit validation flow.

As a farmer, I want OCR on uploaded documents so key fields are prefilled in reports.



Acceptance Criteria:

- OCR extracts core fields: document type, date, and identifier/reference.

- User can review and edit extracted values before saving.

- Low-confidence extraction is visibly flagged for manual confirmation.

As a farmer, I want to upload photo/PDF evidence to a task so that I can prove compliance during checks.



Acceptance Criteria:

- User can attach photo or PDF up to configured size limit.

- Attachment is visible in task detail and report preview.

- Failed upload shows clear error and retry option.

As a system administrator, I want key user actions logged so that actions are traceable for compliance and support. AC: login/profile/report/sync events logged with user and timestamp; logs visible for debug/demo.
As a farmer, I want reminders for upcoming deadlines so that I do not miss compliance dates. AC: reminder before due date; tap opens task detail; reminders can be disabled.

column1: Save Report Draft Offline and Sync Later
column30: As a farmer, I want to save report drafts offline and sync later so that poor connectivity does not block me. AC: save and reopen draft offline; queue unsent actions; auto-sync on reconnect with visible sync result.

As a farmer, I want to submit one basic compliance report so that I can complete required reporting digitally. AC: required fields validated; successful submit shows confirmation; item appears in history.
As a farmer, I want plain-language instructions for each task so that I can complete compliance correctly. AC: what/when/why shown; non-legal language; backend-configurable content.
As a farmer, I want to see my compliance tasks and deadlines so that I know what to do next. AC: list shows title/due date/status; overdue highlighted; content reflects profile rules.
As a farmer, I want to enter my farm details so that the app can show relevant compliance tasks. AC: create/edit profile; required field validation; local save and backend sync.
As a farmer, I want to log in securely so that I can access my compliance workspace. AC: valid login opens dashboard; invalid credentials show clear error; session restores after app restart.
Log key actions (login, profile update, report submit, sync) with user and timestamp.
Send reminders for upcoming deadlines and deep-link to task details.
Support offline draft save, sync queue, retry, and basic conflict policy.
Enable one basic compliance report submission with confirmation and history.
Show personalized compliance tasks with due dates and plain-language guidance.
Capture and sync farm profile data used for compliance task personalization.
Implement secure login/logout and session persistence for farmers.
Establish Android app architecture and core navigation for MVP. Scope: project setup, navigation skeleton, global loading and error states, basic telemetry hooks.

column1: Market analysis, competitor and supplier analysis
column30:
h2. *User Story*

As a product team, I want to conduct a thorough market analysis, competitor analysis, and supplier analysis for the Regulatory Compliance Navigator platform, so that we can validate the market opportunity, understand the competitive landscape in EU agricultural compliance, and identify key technology/data suppliers needed to build our solution.

----

h2. *Context*

Our platform — the Regulatory Compliance Navigator — is an integrated software platform that automates CAP (Common Agricultural Policy) compliance reporting and monitoring across EU Member States. Think "TurboTax for agricultural sustainability." The market analysis should focus on:

* EU-wide CAP compliance market (€98B in green economy incentives, 2023-2027)
* 28% of European farmers receive penalties for non-compliance (Swiss study, 2024)
* Up to 60% of administrative burden deemed unnecessary (EU CAP Network Study, 2025)
* ~150,300 holdings in Lithuania (pilot market), 45% small farms under 5 hectares

----

h2. *Scope*

h3. 1. Market Analysis

* Total Addressable Market (TAM): EU-wide agricultural compliance software/services market across all 27 Member States
* Serviceable Addressable Market (SAM): Countries with similar digital infrastructure to Lithuania
* Serviceable Obtainable Market (SOM): Lithuania as pilot market
* Market trends: EU simplification efforts (2024-2025 emergency measures), digital agriculture adoption, satellite-based monitoring (e.g., CAPCON in Lithuania)
* Target customer segments: small/medium farms, agricultural consultants, National Paying Agencies

h3. 2. Competitor Analysis

* Direct competitors: existing agricultural compliance platforms in the EU
* Indirect competitors: agricultural consultants, general farm management software (FMS), government portals
* Feature comparison matrix (compliance automation, multi-language, cross-Member-State support, real-time alerts)
* Pricing models and market positioning of competitors
* Competitive advantage: our interdisciplinary approach (informatics + communication studies), human-system interaction focus

h3. 3. Supplier Analysis

* Data suppliers: EU regulatory databases, LPIS/IACS systems, satellite monitoring providers
* Technology suppliers: cloud hosting, translation APIs, analytics engines
* Integration partners: National Paying Agencies, existing farm management systems
* Risks and dependencies for key suppliers

----

h2. *Acceptance Criteria*

✅ TAM/SAM/SOM estimated with data sources cited
✅ At least 5 competitors identified and analyzed in a comparison matrix
✅ Competitor strengths, weaknesses, and pricing documented
✅ Key technology and data suppliers identified with cost estimates where possible
✅ Supplier risks and dependencies mapped
✅ Market trends and opportunities summarized
✅ Final report/deliverable added to Confluence or shared in Jira

----

h2. *Definition of Done (DoD)*

✅ Epic connected
✅ Points for the story given
✅ All sub-tasks completed
✅ Acceptance criteria met
✅ Market analysis reviewed and approved by team
✅ Deliverable linked in Jira
✅ Story marked as Done in Jira

column1: Updated questionnaire for interview of farmers
column30:
h2. *Updated User Story*

As a product team, I want to conduct semi-structured qualitative interviews with farmers to deeply understand CAP (SMR/GAEC) compliance pain points and validate whether our solution addresses real needs. So we can check problem-solution fit and gauge willingness to pay.

----

h2. *Interview Script*

h3. *Opening (2 minutes)*

"Thank you for taking the time to speak with me today. My name is [OUR TEAM MEMBER], and I'm a student at KTU working on a project about agricultural compliance.

We're exploring how farmers experience CAP regulations and reporting requirements. There are no right or wrong answers—I'm just interested in learning from your experience. This conversation should take about 15-20 minutes.

Everything you share will be kept confidential, and we won't attribute any specific comments to you by name. Are you comfortable if I take notes during our conversation? [If recording: May I record this for my notes? The recording will only be used internally and deleted after transcription.]

Do you have any questions before we begin?"

----

h3. *Semi-Structured Interview Guide (15-17 minutes)*

*Section 1: Farm Context & CAP Engagement (3-4 minutes)*

*Q1: Tell me about your farm.*

* _Probe:_ What do you grow/produce? How large is your operation?
* _Probe:_ How long have you been farming?

*Q2: How does the CAP fit into your farm's operations?*

* _Probe:_ Do you currently receive CAP payments? For how long?
* _Probe:_ What made you decide to participate (or not participate)?

----

*Section 2: Compliance Experience (6-8 minutes)*

*Q3: Walk me through what happens when you need to handle CAP compliance and reporting. What does that process look like for you?*

* _Probe:_ How much time does it typically take?
* _Probe:_ What's the most time-consuming part?
* _Probe:_ Do you handle this yourself or does someone help you?

*Q4: When you think about SMR and GAEC requirements, what comes to mind?*

* _Probe:_ Which specific requirements do you find most confusing or difficult?
* _Probe:_ Can you give me an example of something that was particularly hard to understand or implement?

*Q5: Have you ever had any issues with compliance—warnings, penalties, or close calls?*

* _If yes:_ Can you tell me what happened?
* _If no:_ Do you worry about this possibility? Why or why not?

*Q6: How confident do you feel about your understanding of all the requirements?*

* _Probe:_ Where do you go when you have questions?
* _Probe:_ What would help you feel more confident?

----

*Section 3: Current Solutions & Workarounds (3-4 minutes)*

*Q7: What tools, systems, or people do you currently rely on to manage compliance?*

* _Probe:_ How well do these work for you?
* _Probe:_ What's missing?

*Q8: Do you work with any external consultants or advisors for CAP reporting?*

* _If yes:_ What do they help with? What does that cost you annually?
* _Probe:_ Is this cost a burden for your farm?

----

*Section 4: Solution Interest & Willingness to Pay (2-3 minutes)*

*Q9: Imagine there was a digital platform that automatically monitored your compliance with SMR and GAEC requirements and alerted you about potential issues before they became problems. How would something like that fit into your farm?*

* _Probe:_ What would be most valuable to you?
* _Probe:_ What concerns would you have?

*Q10: If such a platform could save you [X hours per month based on their Q3 answer] and reduce your risk of penalties, what would that be worth to you?*

* _Probe:_ Would you be willing to pay a monthly fee for something like that? What range seems reasonable?
* _Probe:_ How does that compare to what you currently spend on consultants or your own time?

----

h3. *Closing (1 minute)*

"Thank you so much for sharing your experiences with me. This has been incredibly helpful.

Before we finish—is there anything else about CAP compliance or farm management that we haven't discussed that you think is important for me to understand?

[Listen]

Great. If we have any follow-up questions as we develop this further, would it be okay to reach out to you again?

Thank you again for your time and insights. Best of luck with this season."

----

h2. *Updated Acceptance Criteria*

✅ Interview guide contains clear, open-ended questions organized by theme
✅ Opening and closing scripts are prepared and natural-sounding
✅ Interview can be completed in 20 minutes or less
✅ Guide allows flexibility for follow-up questions based on responses
✅ Pilot test completed with at least one farmer
✅ Interview notes template created for consistent documentation
✅ Link to interview guide and notes template added in Jira

----

h2. *Definition of Done (DoD)*

✅ Epic connected
✅ Points for the story given
✅ All sub-tasks completed
✅ Acceptance criteria met
✅ At least 1 pilot interview conducted and documented
✅ Interview guide reviewed and approved by team
✅ Notes template tested and refined based on pilot
✅ Story marked as Done in Jira

Big Goal: Use structured interviews with farmers to confirm the CAP (SMR/GAEC) compliance issue and find out how much people want a digital compliance monitoring platform.

Scope:Making the questionnaire

Conducting the interview

Analysis of problem validation

Assessment of willingness to pay

Result: Clear proof that the burden of complying with CAP is big enough to warrant the creation of a digital solution.

column1: Prepare for questionnaire for interview of farmers
column30:
h3. User Story

As a product team, I want to use structured interviews with farmers to make sure that CAP (SMR/GAEC) compliance pain points are real.
So we can check the fit between the problem and the solution and see if people are prepared to pay.

1. Farm profile

*What is the size of your farm?*

* Under 10 hectares
* 10–50 hectares
* 50–200 hectares
* Over 200 hectares

h3. 2. Engagement with CAP

*Do you currently receive CAP direct payments?*

* Yes
* No
* Planning to apply

h3. 3. Administrative burden

*How many hours per month do you spend on CAP-related documentation and compliance tasks?*

* Less than 5 hours
* 5–10 hours
* 10–20 hours
* More than 20 hours

h3. 4. Perceived complexity

*How would you rate the complexity of SMR and GAEC requirements?*


(1 = Very simple, 5 = Extremely complex)

h3. 5. Fear of penalties

*Have you ever received a warning or penalty related to CAP compliance?*

* Yes
* No

If yes:
How concerned are you about future penalties? (1–5 scale)

h3. 6. Understanding of regulations

*Do you feel confident that you fully understand all CAP compliance requirements?*

* Yes, completely
* Partially
* No

h3. 7. Use of consultants

*Do you use external consultants for CAP reporting?*

* Yes, regularly
* Occasionally
* No

If yes:
Approximately how much do you spend annually on consulting services?

h3. 8. Current tools

*What tools do you currently use to manage compliance?*

* Paper documentation
* Excel
* Government portals
* Specialized software
* None
